title: Your Gut-Brain Health Action Plan
description: Build your personalized gut-brain health plan based on what you've learned in this course. Check off the strategies you commit to implementing and use the text fields to customize your plan.
instructions: Review each evidence-based strategy and check the ones you plan to incorporate into your daily life. Use the text fields to personalize your commitments. Revisit this plan weekly to track your progress.

items.label: Increase dietary fiber to support SCFA production (target: 25-35g daily)
Include at least one fermented food daily (yogurt, kefir, sauerkraut, kimchi)
Aim for 30+ different plant foods per week for microbiome diversity
Limit ultra-processed foods to 4 or fewer servings daily
Combine protein, fat, and fiber at meals to stabilize blood sugar
Eat regular meals to avoid long gaps that spike cortisol
Include omega-3 rich foods at least 2-3 times per week
Ensure adequate B vitamin intake (leafy greens, eggs, legumes)
Include magnesium-rich foods (dark chocolate, nuts, seeds, leafy greens)
Practice daily stress management (stress disrupts the gut-brain axis)
Prioritize 7-9 hours of sleep (poor sleep disrupts gut bacteria)
Get regular physical activity (exercise improves microbial diversity)
My first change this week will be:
items.placeholder: e.g., Add one serving of fermented food daily

items.label: Rate your confidence in making these changes (1-10)

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational purposes only. All dietary changes should be gradual and sustainable. If you have dietary restrictions, food allergies, or an eating disorder, consult a healthcare provider before making changes. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
